[File: 'File Name: src/TroveManager.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: hasPendingRewards / _redistributeDebtAndColl] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (borrower or liquidator) under PRECONDITIONS where a liquidation produces a collToRedistribute so small that BNBRewardPerUnitStaked rounds to zero (BNBNumerator / totalStakes == 0) while USDSDebtRewardPerUnitStaked > 0 trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [liquidate small-coll trove -> _redistributeDebtAndColl(_coll near 0, _debt > 0) -> L_USDSDebt increases but L_BNB unchanged -> hasPendingRewards returns false for all troves because rewardSnapshots[borrower].BNB == L_BNB -> _applyPendingRewards skipped -> Troves[borrower].debt never incremented by pendingUSDSDebtReward], violating INVARIANT that every active trove's recorded debt must include all pending redistribution rewards before any collateral withdrawal or ICR check, causing scoped impact: borrower's debt is permanently understated, allowing withdrawColl or closeTrove to extract BNB collateral without repaying the full redistributed USDS debt, creating bad debt at protocol expense? [File: 'File Name: src/TroveManager.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: _updateLastFeeOpTime / decayBaseRateFromBorrowing / redeemCollateral] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (borrower + redeemer) under PRECONDITIONS where multiple operations occur within a 60-second window trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [attacker calls openTrove -> decayBaseRateFromBorrowing called -> _updateLastFeeOpTime does NOT update lastFeeOperationTime because timePassed < 60 -> attacker calls redeemCollateral in same block or within 60 seconds -> _calcDecayedBaseRate uses same lastFeeOperationTime -> minutesPassed = 0 -> decayFactor = DECIMAL_PRECISION -> no decay applied -> baseRate remains elevated from prior redemptions -> attacker pays higher fee than expected], violating INVARIANT that baseRate must decay monotonically with time, causing scoped impact: while this primarily affects fee accuracy, an attacker who controls block timing can exploit the 60-second granularity to avoid baseRate increases between rapid sequential redemptions, paying floor fee only when baseRate should have increased? [File: 'File Name: src/TroveManager.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: _redeemCollateralFromTrove / _redeemCloseTrove] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (redeemer) under PRECONDITIONS where a trove's debt equals usdsGasCompensation + 1 wei trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [attacker calls redeemCollateral targeting a trove with debt = gasComp + 1 -> USDSLot = min(maxUSDS, debt - gasComp) = 1 -> BNBLot = 1 * DECIMAL_PRECISION / price rounds to 0 -> trove's coll decreases by 0, debt decreases by 1 -> newDebt = gasComp -> _redeemCloseTrove called -> gasComp USDS burned from GasPool -> newColl = entireTroveColl - 0 = entireTroveColl -> collSurplusPool.accountSurplus(borrower, entireTroveColl) -> borrower claims full collateral for free], violating INVARIANT that a redeemer must pay BNB-equivalent value for every USDS redeemed, causing scoped impact: attacker redeems 1 wei USDS, pays 0 BNB (BNBLot rounds to 0), closes the trove, and the trove owner receives full collateral as surplus while the protocol loses the 1 wei USDS debt without receiving any BNB?
